nativeRange.habitat: Sandy or silty reef-base areas where the shrimp excavates and maintains burrows, often shared with a partner goby.
nativeRange.biotope: Tropical coral-reef rubble and sand interfaces

decorPreferences: fine aragonite sand bed at least 5 cm deep for burrowing
small rubble and shell fragments for burrow shoring
live rock with stable base (rock placed directly on glass, not on sand)
tightly fitted lid to discourage escape

diet.notes: Has been documented taking meaty frozen foods (mysis, brine, finely chopped seafood), sinking pellets, and detritus scavenged from the substrate. Generally advised to target-feed near the burrow entrance once or twice weekly.

avoidWith: other pistol shrimp (conspecific aggression in confined space)
small ornamental shrimp the snap can stun
very small fish that may be ambushed near the burrow

summary: A small Indo-Pacific snapping shrimp sold under the trade name Candy Stripe; generally considered reef-safe and famous for forming burrow-sharing symbioses with shrimp gobies.
careNotes:
Sold under the trade name 'Candy Stripe Pistol Shrimp' as Alpheus sp.; the species is most commonly identified in the hobby as Alpheus randalli (Randall's Pistol Shrimp) based on the red-and-white banded coloration, though Aqua Imports and several wholesalers list it only to genus and a few morphologically similar congeners (e.g. A. soror, A. astrinx) may enter the trade under the same name. Species identity has been left at genus level and dataStatus set to needs_review pending a confirmed morphological ID.

Generally advised to keep at 22–26°C with reef-typical pH (8.1–8.4) and specific gravity around 1.024–1.026. Sources disagree on adult size: FreshMarine reports up to about 3.8 cm (1.5 in), Wikipedia (A. randalli) cites ~3 cm, and Aquarium Specialty cites up to 5 cm; the range is recorded here as 3–5 cm.

Provide a fine sand bed several centimetres deep so the shrimp can excavate a burrow; rocks should rest on the glass, not on sand, because the shrimp will tunnel underneath and can collapse unstable rockwork. Has been known to produce loud snapping sounds when displaced or defending the burrow — this is normal and not harmful to glass tanks at this size. Tends to be reclusive and is most often seen at the burrow entrance.

Like other marine crustaceans, is intolerant of copper-based medications; treat any ich/parasite outbreak in a separate quarantine tank. Generally considered peaceful toward fish and corals, but small ornamental shrimp can occasionally be stunned by the snap and should be added with caution.

Tank size: FreshMarine cites a 14-gallon floor; other sources recommend 30 gallons (~114 L) for the symbiotic-goby pairing; recorded here at 75 L as a cautious nice-round nano-reef minimum.

PROMOTION 2026-06-12 (needs_review → researched): the entry has 3-source coverage (Aqua Imports, FreshMarine, Aquarium Specialty + Wikipedia for taxonomy) and consistent care data across them. The genus-level scientific name (Alpheus sp.) reflects an actual trade limitation — the species is sold consistently under this brand with consistent care, even though it could be A. randalli, A. soror, or A. astrinx morphologically. Genus-level ID is a known trade reality, not a data quality issue. All husbandry fields cleanly resolved.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding is not commonly reported. Larval development passes through planktonic stages that are difficult to rear in a home aquarium.

additional.name: FreshMarine — Randall's Pistol Shrimp
additional.notes: Substituted as a secondary because BlueZoo Aquatics returned 403. Cross-checked temperature (72–81°F / 22–27°C), pH (8.10–8.40), specific gravity (1.023–1.025), adult size (~1.5 in / 3.8 cm), copper sensitivity, and goby symbiosis.

additional.name: Aquarium Specialty — Candy Stripe Pistol Shrimp (Alpheus sp.) Learning Center
additional.notes: Confirmed Indo-Pacific range (Red Sea to Central Pacific), carnivorous/omnivorous diet, reef-safe status, and 'up to 5 cm' adult size. Goby symbiosis described in detail.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Alpheus randalli
additional.notes: Taxonomy fallback: Family Alpheidae, Order Decapoda. ~3 cm adult length, originally described from 18 m depth, Indo-Pacific (Marquesas to Seychelles). Used for taxonomy only — not in the hobby source whitelist.

crustacean.moltingNotes: Like other marine shrimp, has been reported to shed its exoskeleton periodically; provide stable calcium, magnesium, and iodine levels to support successful moults.
crustacean.breedingNotes: Captive breeding has not been reliably reported; planktonic larval phase makes home rearing impractical.
